title: Clearbridge Mid Cap Strategy’s Views on Valvoline (VVV)
content:
ClearBridge Investments, a global equity manager, recently published first-quarter 2026 commentary for its “Mid Cap Strategy”. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. Mid-cap stocks showed strong performance in Q1, with the Russell Midcap Index rising 1.3%, outperforming both large-cap and small-cap stocks. In contrast, large-cap equities dropped 4.2%, while small-caps increased by only 0.9%; this dynamic suggested broadening in market leadership. Though investor concerns about the US-Israel-Iran conflict influenced mid-cap stocks, their performance was more focused on company fundamentals and sector trends. However, the ClearBridge Mid Cap Strategy underperformed its benchmark due to challenges in the IT, health care, and consumer discretionary sectors. The Strategy focuses on the consumer discretionary sector, acquiring firms that can perform in a variety of circumstances while being adaptable as the environment changes. Please review the Strategy’s top five holdings to gain insights into their key selections for 2026.

In its first-quarter 2026 investor letter, ClearBridge Mid Cap Strategy highlighted stocks like Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV). Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) is a US-based automotive preventive maintenance company that offers oil changes, battery, bulb, and wiper replacements; tire rotations; and other maintenance services. On April 9, 2026, Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) closed at $34.81 per share. One-month return of Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) was 3.60%, and its shares gained 2.35% over the past 52 weeks. Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) has a market capitalization of $4.43 billion.

ClearBridge Mid Cap Strategy stated the following regarding Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) in its Q1 2026 investor letter:

"In more idiosyncratic recovery situations, Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV), the largest quick lube operator in North America, provides a differentiated consumer-facing business with defensive growth characteristics. Its demand profile is supported by stable miles driven, a consumer preference shift away from dealerships and a favorable mix shift toward higher-value synthetic oil changes."Why Valvoline Inc (VVV) Is Skyrocketing So Far In 2025?

Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) is not on our list of 40 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds Heading Into 2026. According to our database, 32 hedge fund portfolios held Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) at the end of the fourth quarter, compared to 40 in the previous quarter. While we acknowledge the potential of Valvoline Inc. (NYSE:VVV) as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: A Look At Valvoline (VVV) Valuation As Shares Cool After Recent Gains
content:
Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Recent performance snapshot for Valvoline

With no single headline event setting the tone, Valvoline (VVV) has been drawing attention through its recent share performance and fundamentals, including a last close of $34.09 and a market cap near $4.3 billion.

Over the past month the stock shows a return of about an 11% decline, while the past 3 months reflect roughly a 17% gain. Year to date, total return stands near 18%, with a 1-year total return of about a 5% decline.

See our latest analysis for Valvoline.

Recent trading points to cooling momentum in the short term, with a 30 day share price return of about an 11% decline, set against a 5 year total shareholder return of roughly 33%, so longer term holders have still seen gains overall.

If Valvoline’s move has you reassessing where you want growth potential and resilience, this is a good moment to scan for other opportunities across 20 top founder-led companies

With shares cooling after recent gains, along with solid annual revenue and net income growth and a value score near the high end, is Valvoline quietly undervalued here, or is the market already pricing in its next phase of growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 18.1% Undervalued

With Valvoline last closing at $34.09 against a narrative fair value of $41.60, the widely followed view sees a meaningful gap to that estimate, built on specific assumptions about how its service model can scale.

The consistent growth in same-store sales, driven by both increased transactions and higher ticket sizes from premium service offerings, suggests the company continues to benefit from a durable trend of more vehicles on the road aging and requiring ongoing maintenance. This supports stable and potentially growing revenue and earnings over the long term.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what has to happen for that valuation to hold up? The narrative leans on faster earnings expansion, healthier margins, and a richer mix of premium services over time.

Result: Fair Value of $41.60 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this upside view still depends on internal combustion engine demand holding up, and on wage or labor cost pressures not squeezing service bay margins more than expected.

Find out about the key risks to this Valvoline narrative.

Another lens on valuation

The analyst narrative points to an 18.1% gap to fair value, but the current P/E of 48.9x tells a different story. It is well above the US Specialty Retail average of 19.4x, as well as the 40.2x fair ratio, which suggests investors are already paying up heavily for growth. Where does that leave your margin of safety?
